metadata.title: From Discovery to Access: Towards accessible digital audio-visual cultural heritage archive

authors.author: Karthikeya Puttur Venkatraj, Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica | Delft University of Technology, karthikeya.venkatraj@cwi.nl
Abdallah El Ali, Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica | Utrecth University, aea@cwi.nl
Pablo Cesar, Centrum Wiskunde & Informatica | Delft University of Technology, garcia@cwi.nl
Alessandro Bozzon, Delft University of Technology, A.Bozzon@tudelft.nl

logo.alt: National Institute of Sound & Vision logo

abstract.heading: Abstract
abstract.paragraph: This research focuses on how to create inclusive and engaging audio-visual experiences for cultural heritage archives. By examining the current barriers to accessing and exploring large audi-visual collections and identify how these barriers limit meaningful engagement with cultural content Building on these insights, the project aims to develop and evaluate approaches to accessiblity leveraging Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Immersive media technologies to support richer forms of discovery, navigation and interpretation. By combining participarty design methods with technical innovation, this research aims to provide both practical guidelines and novel concepts that demonstrate how digital archives can be designed with universal access fundamentally embedded within the interface rather than an afterthought.

section.heading: Background
section.paragraph: Digitisation has a massive impact on the way heritage material is preserved, accessed and experienced. In the case of audio-visual collections from the Netherlands Institue of Sound & Vision, a large portion of the collection that was stored on fragile carriers such as film, magnetic tape, and video tapes has been digitised over the past fifteen years and can now be accessed remotely. The Sound & Vision collection chronicles historical events and illuminates the intricate relationship between media, society, and technological progress. Access to this collection is essential for citizens with disabilities to engage with their cultural heritage to foster a more inclusive society.

image.alt: Three illustrated people using digital devices, including a smartphone and laptop, with cloud, gear, and Wi‑Fi icons above them, suggesting inclusive and connected online participation

list.item: Co-design and evaluate accessible AI and immersive meadia tools for cultural heritage with people who have lived experience of disability
Focus on discovery, navigation and interpretation of audio-visual content
Aim for practical, engaging and meaningful experiences that improve inclusion and agency

section.heading: Timeline / Next steps

image.alt: Timeline chart showing project phases from year 1 through year 4. In year 1 & 2, codesign activities are planned with blind and low vision people followed by prototyping, evaluation and deriving guidelines, these activites repeate interatively. In year 3 & 4, codesign activities are planned with deaf and hard or hearing people followed by prototyping, evaluate and derive guidelines, , these activites repeate interatively. Finally ending with the dissertation

section.heading: From co-design to scalable technology for inclusive archival access
